'''Lewis Murphy''' (born 24 March 2002) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]].

==Playing career==
===Wakefield Trinity===
Murphy signed a professional contract with Wakefield in July 2021 having come through their academy and impressing at U19 level.<ref>{{Cite web|url= https://wakefieldtrinity.com/blog/2021/07/21/murphy-signs-two-year-contract-with-first-team|title= Murphy signs two-year contract with First Team|website=Wakefield Trinity}}</ref>
Murphy made a quick impression in his first season, scoring seven tries in his first 13 appearances in the Super League.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hell-be-a-superstar-wakefield-trinitys-lewis-murphy-tipped-for-huge-future-3787914|title= 'He'll be a superstar': Wakefield Trinity's Lewis Murphy tipped for huge future|website=Yorkshire Evening Post}}</ref> He scored a [[hat-trick]] for Wakefield against [[Hull F.C.]] on 19 August 2022<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/62582799|title=Super League: Hull FC 18-26 Wakefield - Treble for winger Lewis Murphy|website=BBC Sport}}</ref> including an acrobatic first, in a triumph which all but secured his relegation threatened club with their Super League status for the 2023 season.<ref>{{Cite web|url= https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-18-26-wakefield-trinity-one-step-closer-to-survival-as-lewis-murphy-scores-hat-trick/|title= Hull 18-26 Wakefield: Trinity one step closer to survival as Lewis Murphy scores hat-trick|website=loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>
In round 26 of the 2022 Super League season, Murphy scored four tries for Wakefield in their 34-18 victory over an understrength [[St Helens RFC]] team.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-18-wakefield-trinity-34-lewis-murphy-shines-as-saints-celebrate-league-leaders-shield-triumph-3823282/|title=St Helens 18 Wakefield Trinity 34: Lewis Murphy shines as Saints celebrate League Leaders' Shield triumph|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}}</ref>
On 7 March 2023, Murphy was ruled out for the remainder of the 2023 Super League season with an ACL injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64873685|title=Wakefield Trinity winger's season over after ACL injury|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===Sydney Roosters===
On 28 April 2023, it was announced that Murphy had signed a contract with the [[Sydney Roosters]] in the [[National Rugby League]] for the 2024 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.standard.net.au/story/8175802/eels-sign-davey-from-dogs-roosters-land-english-flyer/?cs=10269/|title=Eels sign Davey from Dogs, Roosters land English flyer|website=www.standard.net.au}}</ref>

===St Helens===
On 3 September 2024 after failing to make an NRL appearance for the Sydney Roosters, Murphy signed a two-year contract to join English side [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] ahead of the 2025 Super League season. Murphy made his club debut for St Helens in round 1 of the 2025 Super League season and scored a try in their 82-0 victory over Salford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/13209064/lewis-murphy-st-helens-sign-former-wakefield-trinity-winger-from-sydney-roosters-for-2025/|title=Wakefield Trinity winger from Sydney Roosters for 2025|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=3 Sep 2024|title=Saints sign winger Murphy from NRL for 2025|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/czjy8yknjnxo}}</ref>
On 3 June 2025, it was announced that Murphy would miss at least eight weeks with a quad injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.saintsrlfc.com/2025/06/03/injury-update-jack-welsby-lewis-murphy/|title=Injury update: Jack Welsby & Lewis Murphy|website=www.saintsrlfc.com}}</ref>

==Style of play==
Former Wakefield Trinity coach [[Willie Poching]] described Murphy as not the biggest player to play on the wing but his main attributes are his speed and agility.<ref>{{Cite web|url= https://www.totalrl.com/wakefield-winger-making-encouraging-strides-poching/|title=Willie Poaching pleased by progress of Lewis Murphy as Wakefield eye signing|website=Totalrl.com|date=8 May 2022 }}</ref> He has been described as possessing “pace to burn” with the athleticism to be a threat in the air and the potential to cover several roles in a squad.<ref>{{Cite web|url= https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-transfers-2023-rugby-7460433|title= Four players from potentially relegated sides who would improve Hull KR in 2023|website=Hulldailymail.co.uk|date= 14 August 2022}}</ref>
